title: Twitter's content warning algo, equity audits, politicians trading stocks, Fed's next move, mRNA & more
description:
(0:00) Bestie Intro: Sweater update, NYC stories

(8:30) Sacks got flagged on Twitter, theorizing on Twitter's content warning algorithm

(14:37) "Racial equity audits" with product-level consequences might be coming to Big Tech

(27:37) Elizabeth Warren vs. Elon on Twitter, birth of "Senator Karen," understanding where the Fed can go from here

(42:56) Omicron spreading rapidly in NYC, but deaths are still relatively flat, anticipating the quality of life split for blue/red states going forward

(53:02) Jeremy Strong's New Yorker profile and backlash, politicians trading stocks

(1:08:57) Future of mRNA technology

(1:15:39) SF Mayor London Breed's speech on increasing law enforcement: is she the right person to turn the ship around?

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses Twitter's content warning algorithm, racial equity audits in Big Tech, politicians trading stocks, and the future of mRNA technology. They also touch on the Fed's next move, the Omicron variant, and San Francisco's law enforcement. The hosts share their thoughts on various topics, including the potential consequences of racial equity audits and the implications of politicians trading stocks.

takeaways.text: Twitter's content warning algorithm may be flawed
Racial equity audits could have significant consequences for Big Tech
Politicians trading stocks can be a conflict of interest
The future of mRNA technology is promising
The Fed's next move will have significant economic implications
The quality of life split between blue and red states may widen
San Francisco's law enforcement needs to be reformed

quotes.text: Get ready for the no-buy list
quotes.speaker: Unknown

sentiment.notes: The hosts express concern about the potential consequences of racial equity audits and the implications of politicians trading stocks. They also discuss the challenges facing San Francisco's law enforcement.
